title: Renal Corpuscle (Glomerulus and Bowman Capsule)
brick_title: Histology of the Renal Corpuscle and Renal Tubular System
buzzwords: Most proximal part of the nephron
Where blood is filtered into ultrafiltrate
Capillary tuft inside a double-walled capsule
Has a vascular pole and a tubular pole
Fed by afferent, drained by efferent arteriole
Parietal layer is simple squamous epithelium
Mesangial cells support and modulate capillary flow
Glomerulus plus Bowman capsule together

title: Glomerular Filtration Barrier
brick_title: Histology of the Renal Corpuscle and Renal Tubular System
buzzwords: Three-layer size- and charge-selective sieve
Separates capillary blood from urinary space
Fenestrated endothelium is the innermost layer
Heparan sulfate repels negatively charged albumin
Damaged in diabetes causing proteinuria
Thickened basement membrane in diabetic nephropathy
Visceral epithelial foot processes wrap the GBM
Slit diaphragms bridge interdigitating podocyte pedicels

title: Proximal Convoluted Tubule (PCT)
brick_title: Histology of the Renal Corpuscle and Renal Tubular System
buzzwords: Long tortuous tubules filling most of the cortex
Continuous with parietal capsule at the tubular pole
Simple cuboidal epithelium with central nuclei
Acidophilic pink cytoplasm from abundant mitochondria
Reabsorbs two-thirds of filtered water and electrolytes
Reclaims essentially all glucose and amino acids
Performs hydroxylation of vitamin D
Prominent apical brush border of long microvilli

title: Loop of Henle
brick_title: Histology of the Renal Corpuscle and Renal Tubular System
buzzwords: U-shaped tubular segment dipping into medulla
Concentrates the filtrate by countercurrent design
Descending portion reabsorbs water
Ascending portion reabsorbs sodium chloride
Thick limb lined by simple cuboidal cells
Hairpin between proximal and distal tubules
Thin limbs lined by simple squamous epithelium
Descends into medulla, ascends back to cortex

title: Distal Convoluted Tubule (DCT)
brick_title: Histology of the Renal Corpuscle and Renal Tubular System
buzzwords: Cortical tubule following the ascending limb
Performs limited fine-tuning reabsorption
Simple cuboidal cells smaller than PCT cells
Sparse brush border and fewer organelles
Touches arterioles of its own parent corpuscle
Contributes the macula densa to the JGA
Drains into the collecting duct system
Contacts vascular pole forming juxtaglomerular apparatus

title: Juxtaglomerular Apparatus (JGA)
brick_title: Histology of the Renal Corpuscle and Renal Tubular System
buzzwords: Specialized structure at the vascular pole
Senses tubular flow and arterial pressure
Densely packed tubular cells abut afferent arteriole
Modified smooth muscle cells in the arteriole wall
Activates the renin-angiotensin-aldosterone system
Salt-sensing macula densa from the DCT
JG granular cells secrete renin
Triggered by low pressure to release renin

title: Collecting Duct
brick_title: Histology of the Renal Corpuscle and Renal Tubular System
buzzwords: Final tubular component before the renal pelvis
Collects fluid from many nephrons
Simple cuboidal cells with unusually distinct boundaries
Pale-staining epithelium with sparse microvilli
Site of final water and acid-base regulation
Principal cells respond to ADH and aldosterone
Principal cells house aquaporin water channels
Intercalated cells handle acid-base balance
